chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. S – 628 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on MA No.5377/2023 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 3. For hearing of bail application 13.09.2023 Mr. Amanullah Qazi, Advocate for applicant/accused..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 1. Urgency is granted. 2. Deferred. 3. Through this bail application, applicant/accused Qaim-ud- din, son of Eidan, by caste Mahar, seeks protective bail in Crime No.26 of 2023 under Sections 382, 506-II PPC, registered at Police Station Adilpur, District Ghotki. Learned Counsel for applicant/accused contends that applicant is innocent, has been falsely implicated in this case. He further contends that applicant/accused is ready to appear before the Court concerned along with an application under Section 498 CrPC to prove his innocence, however, he apprehends his immediate arrest as non-bailable warrants has been issued against him; hence, he seeks protective bail. Without touching merits and de-merits of the case, protective bail for a period of seven days w.e.f. 13.09.2023 to 19.09.2023 (both days inclusive) is granted to applicant/accused subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.20,000/- (Rupees twenty thousand) and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. Operation of non- Cr. Bail App. No. S – 628 of 2023 Page 2 of 2 bailable warrants in this case shall remain suspended during aforesaid period of seven days. Applicant/accused is directed to surrender before the Court concerned in the aforementioned period. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of above seven days period or whenever applicant/accused surrenders before the Court concerned, which one occurs earlier.

Applicant/accused is directed to surrender before the Court concerned in the aforementioned period. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of above seven days period or whenever applicant/accused surrenders before the Court concerned, which one occurs earlier. It is, however, made clear that in case applicant/accused fails to surrender before the Court concerned within stipulated period, his bail bond shall stand automatically forfeited. Criminal Bail Application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Abdul Basit
